reasoning: Sulphanilic acid (4-aminobenzenesulfonic acid) is a benzene ring substituted with both an amino group (-NH2) at the para position and a sulfonic acid group (-SO3H). It belongs to the benzenesulfonic acids and derivatives class; however, considering the amino substitution on the benzene ring bearing a sulfonyl group, the most specific direct parent is aminobenzenesulfonamides — though strictly it is an aminobenzenesulfonic acid. The best available label is Aminobenzenesulfonamides as the most specific match for an amino-substituted benzenesulfonyl compound.
